figures.caption: Fig. 2: Stage I: agentic trajectory imagination (digital rehearsal). A central LLM agent orchestrates (i) a vision–language model for prompt construction, (ii) a text-to-video diffusion generator, and (iii) a reasoning-based evaluator that scores candidate videos and produces structured critiques. The agent iteratively refines prompts using feedback and memory until a validated reference navigation video Vgoal is obtained.

Fig. 3: Stage II: FlowDiT execution module. FlowDiT conditions on the validated reference video Vgoal (visual features and motion cues) and the instruction L to predict a horizon-H sequence of velocity commands (vx, vy, ω). The policy executes in a recedinghorizon manner by applying the first action and re-evaluating at the next timestep (with optional live observation It when available).

Fig. 1: Action Agent system overview. Stage I performs agentic trajectory imagination by generating and validating a first-person visual intermediate representation (reference navigation video) from a language instruction and an initial observation. Stage II executes this reference using FlowDiT to produce continuous velocity commands for deployment across multiple robot embodiments.
